summary: Windows install-time dropper fetches and executes encrypted Python payload from hardcoded C2
details: scripts/postinstall.js is a Windows-targeted remote-code dropper. XOR-obfuscated byte arrays at line 13-16 decode to the C2 hostname 'node22.lunes.host' and port '3258'. The script HMAC-authenticates with rolling 5-minute tokens, GETs encrypted Python marshal bytecode from /sync and /go endpoints over plain HTTP, decrypts it with a SHA256 keystream, writes randomized files to %TEMP% (msvc_*.vbs, msl_*.dat, mscf_*.py masquerading as MSVC build artifacts), and launches the payload detached via wscript.exe with windowsHide and detached:true — accompanied by an in-source comment stating the spawn pattern is intended to 'escape npm job object'. The launcher and payload self-delete after execution. Compounding the install-time RCE: the package's sole runtime dependency 'node-fetch-core' is pinned to a mutable master-branch GitHub tarball (https://github.com/Hexa-devy/node-fetch-core/archive/refs/heads/master.tar.gz) controlled by the same author, allowing arbitrary code substitution without a version bump. Installing this package on Windows results in full code execution under the installing user with anti-forensic concealment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
